name: Rush Lake
blurb: Where it all began. The early Agate Cup years were played on the shore at Rush Lake, boards set up in the grass between the cabin and the water.

name: Otter Tail Lake
blurb: The tournament's middle era. Bigger crowds, longer brackets, and more than one final decided as the sun went down over the lake.

name: East Leaf Lake
blurb: A stop along the tournament's travels — East Leaf Lake hosted the Cup for a couple of memorable summers before the move to Island Lake.

name: Island Lake
blurb: The current home of the Poopsie Beanbag Invitational, and where the tradition carries on every July 4th weekend.
